title: Permission denied on /var/run/containers/storage
body:
**Description**

Installing buildah on F27 and executing it for the first time gives a "permission denied".

```
$ buildah from scratch
mkdir /var/run/containers/storage: permission denied
mkdir /var/run/containers/storage: permission denied
exit 1
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Install buildah on a vanilla Fedora 27
2. Run `buildah from scratch`

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
$ rpm -qa|grep buildah 
buildah-0.11-1.git6bad262.fc27.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
$ buildah --version
buildah version 0.11 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="27 (Twenty Seven)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=27
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 27 (Twenty Seven)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:27"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=27
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
$ uname -a
Linux ikki 4.14.14-300.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Jan 19 13:19:54 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
$ cat /etc/containers/storage.conf 
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"

```

comments.body: You have to be root to run buildah.
@rhatdan can't we create a `buildah` group that allows non-root users to run buildah w/o `sudo`-ing (something similar to what `docker` does)?
No there is no client server operation for that.  A better way is to just to setup sudo for the buildah command it would be more secure then doing the docker group thing.

> You have to be root to run buildah.

I've already imagined something like that. I could create a PR that adds a notice to the man page or the README. Would that be alright?
@bkircher that would be super!
Closing this issue.

Also was just hit by this - as a user, it would have saved me a *lot* of time if the program would have just checked how it was being executed at runtime and exited if it was being ran as a non root user with a meaningful exit code and message.

Happy to PR that if you agree.
@rhatdan thoughts?  You can run Buildah as non-root these days we haven't gotten it well documented yet and we're working on that.  A good error message sounds good to me and I'm never against a PR.
@kris-nova Please submit a PR.  @giuseppe Can we make this smarter like Podman and just do the right thing if when run as non-root
this should not happen, I'll look into it.  @kris-nova what version of buildah are you using?
@kris-nova great presentation on TGI Kubernetes 054!  I think it might be something related to Ubuntu, as we probably never tried rootless there :-)  I'll try that
I had to install an updated runc and I got it running on Ubuntu 18.10

Hi, I'm facing the same issue on an Ubuntu 16.04 wih runc version 1.0.1-rc6 and buildah 1.8-dev. 
Are there some more Informations on this? It was working just until yesterday for me. What version of runc did you update to?

> Hi, I'm facing the same issue on an Ubuntu 16.04 wih runc version 1.0.1-rc6 and buildah 1.8-dev.
> Are there some more Informations on this? It was working just until yesterday for me. What version of runc did you update to?

is it caused by an updated runc or have you also updated buildah?

I use the Packages provided by the project atomic PPA. And according to /var/log/dpkg.log, the last update was for buildah. It made a jump from 1.6.1 to 1.8.1.
Runc was not touched as it seems.
Ok I manually build release 1.6, 1.7, 1.7.1 and 1.7.2. The problem appears starting with 1.7.2. The releases prior to 1.7.2 are working fine.
have you also tried last version from master?
Yes, I just tried it. Same problem. as in 1.7.2.
thanks for trying it, I am going to reproduce here.  What commands have you used?
Basically just buildah version, buildah containers and buildah images.
I cannot see this issue locally, can you show the content of `~/.config/containers/storage.conf`?

Of course, it contains only:
>RunRoot = "/run/user/500071028"
GraphRoot = "/home/uidg8755/.local/share/containers/storage"
GraphDriverName = "vfs"
it looks like a config file generated by an old (and broken) version of libpod.  Could you delete `~/config/containers` and try again?
Ah, ok. That did the trick. The storage.conf also has gotten a few more entries. Thank you!
good to hear!  You most likely also got fuse-overlayfs storage configured

According to storage.conf vfs should be used. 

> [storage]
  driver = "vfs"
  runroot = "/tmp/500071028"
  graphroot = "/home/uidg8755/.local/share/containers/storage"
  [storage.options]
    size = ""
    remap-uids = ""
    remap-gids = ""
    remap-user = ""
    remap-group = ""
    ostree_repo = ""
    skip_mount_home = ""
    mount_program = ""
    mountopt = ""
    [storage.options.thinpool]
      autoextend_percent = ""
      autoextend_threshold = ""
      basesize = ""
      blocksize = ""
      directlvm_device = ""
      directlvm_device_force = ""
      fs = ""
      log_level = ""
      min_free_space = ""
      mkfsarg = ""
      mountopt = ""
      use_deferred_deletion = ""
      use_deferred_removal = ""
      xfs_nospace_max_retries = ""

Is there anything missing? 
_buildah mount_ tells me: 
>chown /home/uidg8755/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs: operation not permitted

Same as before this just happens on versions greater than 1.7.1

In rootless mode 
buildah mount should be done in a `buildah umount`

Ok I don't really understand that answer. I'm trying to mount the filesystem of a container, that was created with `buildah from xyz`. For this I'm trying `buildah mount xyz-working-container`. 
Together with the debug option I'm getting this:

> DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "vfs"   
chown /home/uidg8755/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs: operation not permitted

Therefore the question if I'm missing some option in the storage.conf or if there is something completely off on my side.


Edit: A `buildah unshare` prior to mount solved the problem.  Just as mentioned in the documentation for releases starting from 1.7.2. The only thing which is not working rootless atm. is the same as described in issue  [1498 ](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1498) .

```
buildah unshare -- sh -c "buildah mount 4b258341e17c"
buildah unshare -- sh -c "buildah unmount 4b258341e17c"
```

`4b258341e17c` - this is my container, works fine.
